free_text: For the heavy ion experiments, a beam
of 77 MeV 12C4+ ions was obtained from the Michigan
The detector was 14.5 cm long and
consisted of two resistive wire proportional counters
backed by a thin plastic scintillator.
The reaction products were detected and
identified on the focal plane of an Enge split-pole
spectrograph. Position along the focal plane, energy
loss in the front proportional counter, light output
in the scintillator, and time of flight relative to
the cyclotron r.f. were all stored in the computer
for off-line sorting. An additional gate was set on
the energy loss in the back proportional counter to
reduce the recording on tape of unwanted particles.
The spectra were taken in two steps, one for the low
excitation region and one for the high excitation
region. The spectra were calibrated by varying the
spectrograph field to move elastically scattered
12C5+ particles across the detector. Data were also
taken in 1 degree steps from theta(lab) = 5 to 10
degrees using a thinner target which consisted of 125
microg/cm2 of 58Ni evaporated onto a 30 microg/cm2
